_meta.name: Question Classification Rules — Module 4 Configuration
_meta.description: Rules for classifying application form questions into Tiers 1-4 and routing them to the correct answer source. Also includes cover letter generation rules.

prompt_caching.applies_to: All Tier 3, Tier 4, and cover letter API calls
prompt_caching.what_to_cache: System prompt + tone_voice_guide + answer_bank (for Tier 4). These are identical across every call in a batch. Use automatic cache_control.
prompt_caching.expected_savings: ~70% of input tokens become cache reads at 10% of base price

cost_controls.batch_processing: Process all approved applications' questions in a single batch session to maximise cache hit rates. Do not make isolated one-off API calls.
cost_controls.skip_if_unnecessary: If an application form has ONLY Tier 1-2 questions and no cover letter field, make ZERO API calls for that application. The vault handles everything.

tier_1.name: Factual / Personal
tier_1.description: Questions with a single correct answer that comes directly from the candidate's personal data.
tier_1.review_gate_display: Greyed out (auto-approved). Visible but not demanding attention.

tier_2.name: Common Screening
tier_2.description: Questions asked by nearly every employer, where the answer is the same regardless of role.
tier_2.review_gate_display: Greyed out (auto-approved). Visible but not demanding attention.

tier_3.name: Role-Specific
tier_3.description: Questions that require a tailored answer referencing the specific job or company.
tier_3.answer_source: AI-generated using Claude API. Inputs: CV, JD, company dossier, tone guide.
tier_3.review_gate_display: Highlighted — requires your approval before submission.

tier_4.name: Competency / Scenario
tier_4.description: Behavioural or situational questions requiring a structured story from real experience.
tier_4.answer_source: AI-generated from answer_bank.json. Matches question to best STAR story, adapts language to role context.
tier_4.review_gate_display: Highlighted — requires your approval before submission.

tier_1_rules._method: Keyword matching against the patterns below. If the question text matches any pattern, auto-fill from the mapped vault field.

patterns.notes: Always answer 'Yes'. Never elaborate on visa type or expiry. See vault._strategy.
Use ideal range, not minimum. If forced to give a single number, use £30,000.

patterns.answer: London-based roles only. Open to all work setups — remote, hybrid, or office-based.

patterns.notes: Check format required — some forms want DD/MM/YYYY, others MM/DD/YYYY.

special_rules.reason: Visa expiry date is never auto-filled. Route to Review Gate with a manual review flag.

special_rules.notes: Select 'No' or the closest equivalent. If the field is a text box, answer 'No'.
Select 'No' or 'Does not apply'. If only 'Prefer not to say' is available, use that.
Always select 'Prefer not to say' or closest equivalent. If not available and the field is required, select the most neutral option.

tier_2_rules._method: Keyword matching. These are common screening questions where the answer is the same regardless of role.

patterns.answer: Seeking career growth and the opportunity to apply my marketing, business development, and commercial skills in a more dynamic environment.

keywords: how did you find out about
patterns.notes: Dynamically populate with the actual job board name (Indeed, LinkedIn, Reed, etc.) based on where the listing was scraped from.

keywords: why are you leaving your current role
patterns.answer: Seeking career growth and a role that offers broader scope to develop my commercial and strategic skills.

tier_3_rules._method: AI classification. If a question doesn't match any Tier 1 or Tier 2 pattern and is about the specific role or company, it's Tier 3.
trigger_patterns: why do you want to work at [company]
what interests you about this role
what attracted you to this position
what do you know about us
what relevant experience do you have for this position
how does your experience relate to this role
what can you bring to the team
what makes you a good fit

answer_generation.system_prompt: You are answering a role-specific application question for Aafreen Fathima. Write a concise, warm, and genuine answer that connects her real experience to the specific role and company. Use the candidate's CV, the job description, and the company dossier to write something specific — not generic. Follow the tone guide: warm, confident, grounded. Never aggressive or template-sounding. Keep to 100-200 words unless the form specifies a different limit. Never fabricate achievements or claim experience she doesn't have.
answer_generation.word_count_short_field: 50-80 words (if the form field is small or has a low character limit)

tier_4_rules._method: AI classification. Questions asking for a specific example, story, or situation from past experience.
trigger_patterns: tell us about a time you
give an example of a time

answer_generation.system_prompt: You are answering a competency question for Aafreen Fathima. You will receive her Answer Bank (a library of real STAR stories from her work history) and the job description. Your job is to: (1) select the most relevant story from the Answer Bank for this specific question, (2) adapt the language to fit the question wording and the role context, and (3) write a natural, flowing answer using the STAR framework but without making it feel mechanical. The answer should read like a genuine story told with warmth and clarity, not four labelled boxes. Follow the tone guide: warm, grounded, confident. Never invent experience. Never combine stories. Keep to 150-250 words unless the form specifies a different limit. Always base the answer on a real story from the Answer Bank.
answer_generation.story_selection_logic: Match the question's competency theme against the competency_tags in each Answer Bank story. Pick the story with the strongest tag overlap. If multiple stories match equally, prefer the one from the most recent role (Caring Hearts > Welstand > Far Out).

classification_pipeline._description: The classifier runs in this order. It stops at the first match.

steps.action: Check Tier 1 special rules. Visa expiry → flag for manual review. Criminal record → auto-fill 'No'. Disability → auto-fill 'No' or 'Does not apply'. Equality monitoring → auto-fill 'Prefer not to say'.
Check Tier 1 factual patterns. If matched, auto-fill from vault.
Check Tier 2 common screening patterns. If matched, auto-fill from vault.
Check for Tier 4 competency triggers. If the question asks for a specific example, story, or past situation, classify as Tier 4.
steps.method: Keyword matching on trigger_patterns, then AI confirmation if ambiguous

steps.action: If none of the above matched, classify as Tier 3 (role-specific). Generate an AI answer using the JD and company dossier.

classification_pipeline.ambiguity_handling: If a question could be Tier 2 or Tier 3, treat it as Tier 3 (the higher-quality path). Better to over-generate than to give a generic stock answer to something that deserved personalisation.

when_to_generate.optional_cover_letter_field: Generate and include in Review Gate for approval.
when_to_generate.mandatory_cover_letter_upload: Generate, render as PDF matching CV design, include in Review Gate.
when_to_generate.why_do_you_want_to_work_here_field: Treat as Tier 3 question, not a full cover letter. Generate 2-3 concise paragraphs.

generation_config.system_prompt: You are writing a cover letter for Aafreen Fathima. You will receive her tailored CV for this role, the job description, the company dossier, and her tone/voice guide. Follow the cover letter rules from the tone guide exactly: never open with 'I am writing to apply...'. Open with something specific about the company or role. Keep to 3-4 paragraphs, under 300 words. Structure: Paragraph 1 = hook (why this role/company caught her attention). Paragraph 2 = what she brings (most relevant experience with specific results). Paragraph 3 = why the fit makes sense. Paragraph 4 (optional) = warm, confident close. Tone: conversational but professional. Sound like a real person, not a template.

form_field_type_handling._description: Different form field types require different handling strategies.
form_field_type_handling.text_input_short: For single-line text fields: keep answer concise. If Tier 1/2, auto-fill directly. If Tier 3/4, use the short word count variant.
form_field_type_handling.text_input_long: For multi-line text areas: use full answer length. Check for character limits in the field attributes.
form_field_type_handling.dropdown_select: Match the best option from the dropdown values to the vault answer. If no exact match, flag for manual review.
form_field_type_handling.radio_buttons: Same as dropdown — match best option. Yes/No questions: match from vault.
form_field_type_handling.checkbox: Select applicable options. For 'select all that apply' questions, match against vault data.
form_field_type_handling.file_upload: If labelled as cover letter upload: generate and upload the PDF cover letter. If labelled as CV upload: upload the tailored CV PDF.
